data.label: Triage Agent
data.description: Reads the customer's message and decides whether to (a) look up an order via the `lookup_order` tool, and/or (b) collect structured input from a human via the `collect_feedback` tool. Both tools are SubWorkflows wired via the `tools` handle; the agent compiler derives each tool's input schema from its child's Start contract and emits the dispatch/collect transitions automatically. `collect_feedback` is the dynamic-form-as-agent-tool case: the agent DESIGNS the form by passing a `steps` array (TaskStepConfig[]) as the tool args; the child's Start declares `steps` (with the TaskStepConfig[] schema inline, so the LLM is told the exact grammar), a HumanTask renders it via `stepsRef = "start.steps"`, and the operator's answers come back as the tool result.
data.systemPrompt: You are a concise customer-service agent who ALWAYS uses tools rather than guessing. Tools: lookup_order(order_id) fetches the current shipping status for an order id; collect_feedback(steps) renders a form to collect input from the human — DESIGN it by passing a `steps` array where each step is {id, title, blocks} and each block is either {type:"mdsvex", content} for display text or {type:"input", field:{name, label, kind, required}} with kind text|textarea|number|select|checkbox|radio|date. Rules: (1) if the message already contains an order id (e.g. ORD-42), call lookup_order with it immediately; (2) if the customer asks about an order but gives NO order id, you MUST call collect_feedback to ask for it — design a one-field text input named `order_id`; do NOT just ask in plain text, use the tool; (3) after collect_feedback returns answers, read the order id from them and call lookup_order with it; (4) once you have the shipping status, reply in one sentence.

data.label: lookup_order
data.description: The order-lookup tool, realized as a sub-workflow (the 08a-order-lookup child template). The child's Start declares `order_id`, and THAT Start contract is what the agent compiler turns into this tool's LLM-facing input schema — there is no input declaration on this node itself. The child runs a Python lookup and returns `status`/`eta`, mapped back here as the tool result the agent feeds to its next turn.

data.label: collect_feedback
data.description: The human-feedback tool, realized as a sub-workflow (the 09b-collect-feedback child). The child's Start declares `steps` (a TaskStepConfig[] with the form-block schema inline) — THAT Start contract is what the agent compiler turns into this tool's LLM-facing input schema, so the model knows to compose a form. Inside the child, a HumanTask renders the agent-designed form via `stepsRef = "start.steps"`, and the End returns the operator's `answers` as the tool result the agent feeds to its next turn.
